title: Port Knocking
description: A hidden service on `chimera-relay.nexus` only opens port 22 after a port-knock sequence: 7000, 8000, 9000. What series of commands would send the knock sequence using netcat?
solution: nc -z chimera-relay.nexus 7000; nc -z chimera-relay.nexus 8000; nc -z chimera-relay.nexus 9000
hint: Port knocking sends connection attempts (not data) to a sequence of closed ports. Use `nc -z` which just checks if a port is open without sending data.

title: Packet Sniffer
description: You need to capture HTTP traffic on interface `eth0` to find credentials being transmitted in plaintext. What `tcpdump` command captures only HTTP packets (port 80) and saves to a file `capture.pcap`?
hint: tcpdump uses BPF filters. The `-i` flag selects the interface, `-w` writes to a file, and `port 80` filters HTTP traffic.

title: ARP Poisoning
description: To perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the LAN segment, you need to send gratuitous ARP replies to poison the ARP cache of two hosts: gateway (192.168.1.1) and target (192.168.1.50). What arpspoof command intercepts traffic from target to gateway?
hint: arpspoof syntax: `arpspoof -i <interface> -t <target-to-poison> <gateway-to-impersonate>`. You also need to run the reverse direction to complete MITM.

title: DNS Tunneling
description: Exfiltration over DNS is being used to bypass the firewall. Data is encoded in subdomain labels sent to `attacker.c2.io`. The maximum label length is 63 characters and total hostname length is 253. What is the maximum data per query in bytes if using hex encoding (2 chars per byte)?
hint: Available label space = 253 - len('.attacker.c2.io') - dots. With hex encoding, divide usable chars by 2. A single label of 63 chars = 31 bytes of data.
